purpose: Root npm workspace manifest (incl. apps/admin), shared scripts, dev dependencies, and Node engine requirement.
Locked npm dependency graph for all workspaces.
CI pipeline: install, build core/shared/server, typecheck, lint, unit tests, PostgreSQL integration tests, web build, admin build, and Playwright e2e.
Strict shared TypeScript compiler options used by workspace configs.
Root TypeScript project references for workspace typechecking/build coordination.
ESLint 9 flat config with TypeScript recommended rules and React Hooks checks for the web app.
Serial Chromium Playwright e2e configuration; starts built `apps/server/dist/index.js` on 4000 and `apps/web` dev server on 3000, with screenshots/traces on failure/retry.

Environment template for server host/port, DATABASE_URL, PG pools, turn/pacing settings, NODE_ENV, TRUST_PROXY, email (Resend key/from, APP_BASE_URL, CONTACT_EMAIL), optional Google Cloud chat translation, and MP action logging.
Ignore rules for dependencies, build outputs, env files, runtime SQLite data, logs, local tools, and local docs.
npm configuration including engine-strict enforcement.
Pinned Node major version for local/CI runtime selection.
Pinned Node major version for tooling that reads .node-version.
Single app version source read by the web Next config.
Public project overview, architecture summary, screenshots, setup commands, and contribution notes.
Next.js/React workspace manifest and web scripts.
Next config that reads root VERSION into NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_VERSION, transpiles workspace core, and sets Turbopack root.
Single app route that renders AppShell.
Root layout, metadata, viewport, global CSS import, and PWA registration.
Import-only CSS entrypoint; cascade order for feature CSS partials.
Next-generated web app manifest for installable PWA metadata and icons.
Service worker for installable PWA caching/offline behavior; cache version is tied to VERSION/deploy.sh and can mask asset changes if stale.

Client shell for screen routing, locale, audio, selected round count, auth/profile state, stats/balance refresh, the SP coin-reward flow (request token on game start, redeem on game end), session restore, and password-reset hash flow.

Main lobby screen with mode controls, settings/rules/leaderboard/auth/profile surfaces, and round-count selection. Settings dialog: difficulty selector with a single-player-only scope note (difficultyScopeNote); About tab renders app meta + the ContactForm.

Bilingual contact form in the Settings -> About panel: email + message + submit, client-side validation mirroring the server (email regex + 10..2000 chars), POST /contact via contactApi, and localized idle/sending/sent/error status (incl. 429 rate-limit). No auth required.
Client HTTP wrapper for the contact form: apiContact (POST /contact) with email/message/locale. No token. Reuses requestJson/jsonInit from authApi (same server HTTP base).

Global leaderboard dialog: fetches GET /auth/leaderboard, renders a fixed-column grid table (rank/avatar/nickname/badge/W/L/G/win%/lang) with loading/error/empty/ready states and a discriminated self panel; exports the lobby TrophyIcon.
Maps a RankBadgeId to its public badge asset path (/assets/Badges/<id>.svg); web-side asset resolution paired with shared rankToBadge.

Reusable decorative rank-badge overlay (RankBadgeId -> /assets/Badges/<id>.svg) absolutely positioned over an avatar's top-right corner; renders null when no badge. Shared by the lobby profile, MP waiting-room seat, and both MP game tables.

Token-only styles for the AvatarRankBadge overlay (.avatarRankBadge) and the non-clipping .avatarBadgeWrap wrapper; the badge overflows the avatar circle's top-right corner and sits above the clipped circle.
Token-only styles for the leaderboard dialog: shared fixed-column grid template (head/rows/self), nickname ellipsis, vertical scroll with sticky header, and mobile column tightening.
Desktop circular lobby controls and compact lobby panel for constrained viewports.

Single-player game workflow around core state: bidding, AI timers, tile play, trick completion, round end, exit flow, and a once-per-game onGameEnd callback (human placement) that drives the SP coin reward.
Single-player bid, number selection, round summary, game end, and exit dialogs. GameEndDialog also shows a '+N' gold-coin reward banner (coinGainPulse) when an SP award was granted.

Shared modal implementation with dialog semantics and focus behavior. Reads PresenceContext to apply a data-state (open|closing) on the backdrop for centralized enter/exit animations and sets `inert` on the panel during the closing phase (keyboard-safe).
Focus trap, Escape handling, and focus restoration hook for modal dialogs.

Radix-Presence style mount/unmount transition (no library): usePresence(open, exitMs) keeps a component mounted through its CSS exit animation, then unmounts; Presence wrapper caches the last-open child element so data-coupled dialogs ({data && <X data/>}) never render with null data during close; PresenceContext provides status (open|closing) to Dialog. Reduced-motion skips the exit delay. Used at every dialog call site for smooth appear/disappear.
Multiplayer UI orchestrator for lobby, room, chat, dialogs, mobile/desktop lobby views, and game table transition.
Multiplayer table shell that renders authoritative snapshots and sends bid/move intents.
Desktop multiplayer lobby/room/chat surface, including MP lobby chat emoji input and per-message translation affordance.
Phone-portrait multiplayer lobby surface with responsive room list/chat behavior, including MP lobby chat emoji input and per-message translation affordance.
Emoji picker button for the MP lobby chat input (desktop + mobile). Inserts an emoji at the caret without exceeding CHAT_MAX_LENGTH; no protocol/server changes (sends via existing sendChat flow).
Reusable MP lobby chat translation button and translated/error text rendering. Calls the server-side `/chat/translate` API with the current UI locale code as target language.
Client HTTP wrapper for MP lobby chat translation requests to `/chat/translate`.
Derives the HTTP server base URL from the configured MP WebSocket URL; shared by auth and chat translation clients.
Browser-side WebSocket client, reconnect/session handling, protocol messages, and client-view event reduction wiring.
React hook that owns the multiplayer client lifecycle and exposes view/actions to UI components.

Client-side reducer/view model for server events, lobby state, room state, game snapshot, and errors. Phase 4: holds `wallet.balance` from WELCOME `goldBalance` + live `WALLET_UPDATED`; every WELCOME reflects current auth state so an anonymous reconnect clears a stale balance.
Transforms server game snapshots into renderable table view and UI-only playable-tile hints. Phase 4: carries `pot` (from RoomView.pot) for the table pot display.
Phase 4 shared coin badge (icon + entry fee, aria-labeled) for paid rooms in both desktop and mobile room lists; renders nothing for free rooms. Token-only color (--coin).
Phase 4 gold pot indicator on the mobile MP table (MP_MOBILE_POS.pot, mirror of the round counter); renders only when pot > 0. Desktop shows the pot in MpInfoPanel instead.

Client HTTP API wrapper for optional account auth, profile, password reset, avatar upload, account language, and `balance` in the /auth/me response. Exports requestJson/jsonInit reused by the SP reward client.
Client auth-token state, profile loading, logout, refresh, avatar upload, account language, and gold-coin balance state (from /auth/me; optimistic STARTING_COINS on register).
Client-side avatar image validation, canvas crop/resize, and compressed blob preparation before upload.
Locale registry and helpers for English/Latvian UI strings.
Best-effort localStorage/sessionStorage wrappers that avoid crashes when browser storage is unavailable or malformed.
Server workspace manifest, build/migrate/test scripts, and runtime dependencies.

Server composition root: config, storage, auth, gold-coin wallet + SP reward tokens, admin auth/audit (when enabled), room manager, chat, router, gateway, event bus, HTTP server (admin/auth/sp/stats/contact/chat handlers + authRoutes.onLoginAttempt -> login_attempts), WS upgrade, periodic cleanup (incl. admin sessions/codes), shutdown.

Server env/.env loader and validator for ports, DB URL, timing, PG pools, CORS origins, proxy trust, email settings (Resend key/from, APP_BASE_URL, CONTACT_EMAIL recipient), optional chat translation settings, and the admin panel config (ADMIN_PASSWORD_HASH scrypt / ADMIN_EMAIL / ADMIN_WEB_ORIGIN; admin.enabled is derived from the password hash presence).

Raw HTTP server for `/health`, `/metrics`, optional chat translation routes, optional admin routes (`/admin/*`), optional contact-form route (`POST /contact`), optional SP coin-reward routes (`/sp/*`), optional deep-stats route (`/stats`), optional auth routes, and JSON error responses. API handlers are tried in order: admin -> contact -> sp -> stats -> auth.

Optional `/auth/*` HTTP routes with validation, rate limits, bearer auth, CORS allowlist, password reset, avatar upload/serve, the global `GET /auth/leaderboard`, `PATCH /auth/me/language`, the gold-coin signup bonus on register, and `balance` in `GET /auth/me`. Exposes an optional onLoginAttempt hook (fire-and-forget, transport layer supplies IP) used to record player login_attempts for the admin panel. Shares raw-HTTP helpers via http/httpUtils.ts.
Optional account service for register/login/me/logout/profile/stats, account language preference, password reset, and public auth resolution for multiplayer seats.

Global leaderboard service: cached rank snapshot (generation-invalidated, TTL-refreshed) over account stats; builds the top-N + discriminated self response and resolves per-user seat rank badges synchronously.

Email abstraction (console dev sender + Resend production sender) for the password-reset email, the About-panel contact message (sendContactMessage with Resend reply_to=author), and the admin 2FA login code (sendAdminLoginCode, English-only).
Global lobby chat service with trimming, length checks, token-bucket rate limit, bounded history, and persistence callback.
MP lobby chat translation use-case: cache repeated translations and enforce in-memory daily/monthly character budgets before calling a translator provider.
Infrastructure adapter for Google Cloud Translation Advanced v3 using server-only service account credentials.
HTTP `/chat/translate` route with bounded JSON input, language-tag validation, CORS, IP rate limit, quota error mapping, and Google-provider failure handling.

Optional `POST /contact` route for the About-panel contact form: no auth, per-IP rate limit (5/h), CORS allowlist, bounded JSON body (8 KiB), zod validation (email regex + 10..2000 char message), delivery via EmailSender.sendContactMessage to the owner address (CONTACT_EMAIL) with reply_to=author. Mounted only when an EmailSender exists; send failure returns 502 (never silent).
Integration coverage for `POST /contact`: valid send with reply-to + owner recipient, default locale, invalid email/short message rejection (400), non-POST 405, and per-IP rate limit (429).

Admin panel storage capability (separate from AuthStore/CoinStore), implemented by both SqliteStorage and PostgresStorage. Methods for admin sessions (create/get/touch/revoke/deleteExpired), the singleton OTP login code (create=atomic upsert, consume=atomic validate+attempts), audit log (append/list), player login_attempts (append), and Phase 1 player reads (searchPlayers by id/name/email sorted by last successful login, getPlayerLoginHistory, countPlayerLoginAttempts). Exports ADMIN_LOGIN_CODE_ID and the isAdminStore guard.

Admin auth logic (Phase 0): scrypt password verify (reuses auth/passwords.ts), email OTP 2FA (single active challenge, single-use, attempts-capped, TTL'd), session issue/resolve/revoke (sliding TTL). login() is constant-form (no password oracle): both paths run verifyPassword and the email is fire-and-forget off the response critical path. Sessions + OTP stored only as sha256 hash.

Admin audit log writer (Phase 0, section 22). record() appends one row per admin action; list() reads newest-first for the UI. Scope: authenticated admin actions + session lifecycle (admin.login/logout) + security signals (admin.verify_failed); 2FA auth-mechanism internal state is deliberately not audited. Phase 1+ data mutations write before/after diffs.

Admin HTTP routes: auth (/admin/login|verify|logout|session|audit) + Phase 1 player reads (GET /admin/players search, /admin/players/:id overview, /admin/players/:id/logins paginated). requireAdmin guard on all; session cookie HttpOnly+Secure+SameSite=Strict; CSRF double-submit (X-CSRF-Token == admin_csrf cookie) on mutating routes (reads are GET, no CSRF). Per-IP + per-identity rate limit on login/verify; CORS with credentials for ADMIN_WEB_ORIGIN. Mounted only when admin is enabled.

Admin player read service (Phase 1). Composes the player profile overview (account + email + avatar + coin balance + MP user_stats + login summary) from AuthStore + CoinStore + AdminStore via Promise.all; also exposes search and paginated login history. Read-only; mutations (Phase 2) go through separate audited paths.
Admin Players search + list (Phase 1.1): session-guarded; search box (id/name/email) and a table linking to each player, sorted by last successful login (server-side).
Admin player profile (Phase 1.2/1.3): session-guarded; shows account info, email, avatar, coin balance, MP stats, and a paginated login history with failed attempts highlighted (suspicious signal).

Cookie helpers for the admin session: parseCookies + serializeCookie (Path=/, SameSite=Strict, Max-Age, optional HttpOnly/Secure) and the ADMIN_SESSION_COOKIE / ADMIN_CSRF_COOKIE / ADMIN_CSRF_HEADER constants.

Admin HTTP integration coverage: constant-form login (200 regardless of password / email failure), HttpOnly session + readable CSRF cookie issuance on 2FA, wrong-code 401, /admin/session 401-vs-200 guard, logout CSRF 403-vs-200 + session invalidation, audit entry on sign-in and on failed verify, audit/session blocked without a session.
Admin Next.js workspace manifest (port 3001) and scripts (dev/build/start/typecheck). Separate from apps/web; deps mirror web (next 16.2.6, react 19.2.6).

Admin Next.js config; Turbopack root = repo root so monorepo node_modules resolve. App runs on port 3001 and talks to the server (/admin/*) at NEXT_PUBLIC_ADMIN_API_BASE (default http://localhost:4000).

Admin API client: credentials:'include' fetches to /admin/*; mutating requests add X-CSRF-Token from the readable admin_csrf cookie (double-submit). Exposes apiLogin/apiVerify/apiSession/apiLogout/apiAudit and the AuditEntry type.
Admin login page (client): two-step flow password -> 6-digit email OTP. Redirects to /dashboard when a session is already valid or after a successful verify. English-only.
Admin dashboard + Audit History (Phase 0, section 22): session-guarded; renders the audit log table (time/action/target/summary/IP) with an expandable JSON diff, plus sign-out.

WebSocket connection gateway: handshake, protocol parsing, sessions, auth resolution (WELCOME also carries goldBalance when a wallet is configured), hub/fanout integration, supersede handling, backpressure, event delivery, and reconnect behavior.

Post-handshake application router for lobby, room, game, snapshot, resume, chat, membership, ownership, and finalization paths. Phase 3 also orchestrates paid-room money: createPaidRoom/joinPaidRoom (debit-then-commit-seat with rollback; synchronous isUserSeated re-check before commit to close the duplicate-userId race) and leave/delete/TTL refunds via tryRefund + an in-memory pendingRefunds retry queue drained by sweepExpiredRooms.
PostgreSQL LISTEN/NOTIFY-backed cross-instance fanout bus for broadcast/player/supersede messages.

In-memory lobby room metadata, seats, visibility, TTL, room codes, and room summaries/views. Phase 3: Room carries entryFee + pot; Seat carries opaque entry{entryId,payerUserId}; owns pot bookkeeping (create/join +fee, leave −fee, forfeit/mid-game teardown unchanged so forfeited fees stay in the pot). Money-agnostic (no wallet).
Authoritative room workflow coordinator: lobby membership, engine/director creation, game start, snapshots, disconnect/forfeit, teardown, and persistence hooks.
Per-room single-writer state engine around core multiplayer applyCommand, request idempotency, event sequencing, snapshots, and timers.
Server-side game progression helper for bot/timeout pacing that delegates decisions to multiplayer core helpers.
Room ownership guard abstraction and PostgreSQL lease-backed implementation for multi-instance foundations.
Reconnect session manager for player identity, reconnect tokens, display ids, public profiles, durable-session integration, and cleanup.
Async persistence interface for matches, events, stats, chat, auth, avatars, sessions, and leases.

Single DDL source for SQLite/PostgreSQL migration rendering: auth, stats, password reset, custom avatars, user_preferences, the gold-coin wallet (0007), deep player stats (0008_player_game_results), the admin panel (0009_admin: admin_sessions, admin_login_codes [singleton id row for the OTP], admin_audit_log, login_attempts), 0010_coin_ledger_open_reason (drops coin_ledger.reason CHECK; reason now enforced by the TS LedgerReason union), 0011_bans (account/IP bans), 0012_chat_blocked_words (chat moderation), and PG-only multi-instance tables. Migrations are append-only; identical CHECK/FK/types across both dialects.
PostgreSQL forward-only migration runner and exported MIGRATIONS built from schema.ts.

SQLite StoragePort/AuthStore/CoinStore/PlayerStatsStore/AdminStore implementation using node:sqlite and schema migrations; coin applyLedger and admin OTP consume use synchronous transactions; admin OTP create is an atomic singleton upsert.

PostgreSQL StoragePort/AuthStore/CoinStore/PlayerStatsStore/AdminStore implementation with pool config, migrations, health checks, durable sessions, room leases, fanout, an ensure-row + FOR UPDATE transaction for atomic per-user coin ledger writes, and an atomic singleton-upsert + FOR UPDATE flow for the admin OTP login code.
Fire-and-forget match/event/chat persistence coordinator behind StoragePort.
Server-authoritative registered-user multiplayer win/loss outcome recorder.
setTimeout-backed server turn timer scheduler.
Core package manifest exposing root and `./multiplayer` ESM entrypoints.
Authoritative domino tile helpers, trump/ace definitions, full double-six set, and shuffle entrypoint.
Human-style cut-and-overhand shuffle algorithm shared by single-player and seeded multiplayer shuffle.
Player construction, hand mutation helpers, legal-play invalid-reason logic, and round scoring.
Authoritative single-player/shared game state transitions: create, bid, play tile, complete trick, next round, standings, winner.
CPU bid, tile, and declared-number heuristics using core rule comparison.
Central deterministic multiplayer command/state transition entrypoint.
Multiplayer-only game/player/turn state wrappers around core GameState.
Typed multiplayer command contract for core state-machine inputs.
Typed multiplayer event contract emitted by core state-machine transitions.
Privacy-safe public/player snapshot builders; public snapshots omit hands while player snapshots reveal only the viewer hand.
Seeded RNG and deterministic multiplayer domino shuffle.
Read-only legal move helper for multiplayer turn state and UI hints.
Read-only legal bid helper for multiplayer turn state.
Rebuilds multiplayer state from deterministic seed/setup and ordered event stream.
Read-only multiplayer invariant checks used by tests and simulators.
Shared protocol package manifest; builds core first and exposes the shared ESM entrypoint.
Zod validation and TypeScript types for all client-to-server protocol messages.

Zod validation and TypeScript types for server-to-client events and fanout messages; imports core MP event/snapshot types. WelcomeEvent carries an optional `goldBalance` for logged-in users; Phase 3 adds WALLET_UPDATED (balance push) to BOTH the union and serverEventSchema so cross-instance Postgres fanout keeps it (gold-coin economy, additive).
Shared room status/visibility/seat DTOs (incl. optional seat rankBadge) and round-count limits. Phase 3: RoomSummary.entryFee + RoomView.pot (required; 0 = free room).

Global leaderboard protocol + domain: authoritative rank->badge mapping (rankToBadge / RankBadgeId asset ids), GameLanguage list, and the LeaderboardEntry / LeaderboardSelf / LeaderboardResponse DTOs (no internal userId — privacy).
Preset avatar catalog, default avatar, validation, and public asset path helper.
Shared title tier and loser-badge calculations from user stats.
Headless multiplayer full-game simulator that drives applyCommand and asserts invariants.
Local load-test CLI that drives real WebSocket clients, checks `/health`, polls `/metrics`, records load-test metrics, and reports stability/latency/resource data.
Starts disposable Docker PostgreSQL, sets TEST_POSTGRES_DATABASE_URL, runs server PostgreSQL integration tests, and cleans up.
Server Docker image example on Node 24; builds core/shared/server and runs server dist.
systemd service example for the Node multiplayer server.
nginx reverse proxy example for web, `/ws`, `/auth/*`, `/chat/*`, and `/contact` routing.
Caddy reverse proxy example for web, `/ws`, `/auth/*`, `/chat/*`, and `/contact` routing.
Operational backup/restore guidance for PostgreSQL and SQLite.
Keeps local runtime data directory present; SQLite database files here are ignored.
Browser smoke coverage for primary single-player flow.
Browser-to-server multiplayer smoke coverage over real WebSocket/server path.
Primary core rule tests for tiles, shuffle, legal play, scoring, trick resolution, tiebreakers, and AI behavior.
Multiplayer command/state transition coverage for applyCommand.

Shared storage contract suite used to keep SQLite/PostgreSQL behavior aligned (matches/events/stats/chat/outcomes/coin wallet/player game results + the admin store: sessions, singleton OTP consume, audit, login_attempts).
Tracked human-readable Latvian game rules document.
Tracked concise rules summary; implementation/tests are authoritative when docs conflict.
Tracked scoring examples.
Tracked strategy notes for players.
Docker build context exclusions for dependencies, build outputs, env files, runtime data, logs, and local-only files.
Ignored/local VPS deployment script; bumps VERSION, updates service-worker cache version, commits/pushes to main, copies code to VPS, builds production server/web, and restarts services.
Theme identifier/source for the current default web theme and theme-related tests.
Design-token source for default colors, RGB channel pairs (incl. the --coin / --coin-rgb gold alias of --primary), spacing, radius, shadows, motion, and theme overrides.
Shared premium black-glass layer styles driven by theme tokens, including shadow color tokens.
Shared button primitive for web UI commands using the project style contract.
Shared icon-button primitive for compact tool/actions and accessible labels.
Shared text-field primitive for auth/profile/settings forms.
Token-driven CSS for shared Button primitive.
Token-driven CSS for shared IconButton primitive.
Token-driven CSS for shared TextField primitive.
Optional account auth dialog for login/register/reset-entry workflows.
Lobby profile/account surface for authenticated users: avatar, name, title, gold-coin balance (CoinBalance, under the title), and stats. Renders nothing for anonymous/loading.
Password-reset screen reached from URL hash token flow.

Client-side service-worker registration plus new-version update flow: registers with updateViaCache:'none', calls registration.update() on mount and tab focus, and on controllerchange either silently reloads (when reload-safe via reloadGate) or shows a localized soft-update prompt.

Module-level reload-safety signal (set by AppShell from the current screen) plus the pure decideReloadAction() that maps {hadController, reloadSafe} to ignore/reload/prompt for the PWA update flow. Default is unsafe (prompt) until AppShell sets it.
Installable PWA prompt UI and related browser event handling.
Localized rules dialog rendered from shared rules content.
Phone-portrait single-player table layout.
Multiplayer connection/reconnect status banner.
Desktop multiplayer table layout for authoritative game snapshots.
Phone-portrait multiplayer table layout for authoritative game snapshots.

Multiplayer lobby/room dialogs for join/create/error flows. Phase 4: CreateRoomDialog shows the gold entry-fee input only to logged-in hosts (hostBalance defined), with client-side balance validation; onCreate forwards entryFee. Server re-checks at debit.
Mobile multiplayer layout calculations and helpers. Phase 4: adds MP_MOBILE_POS.pot (right column, mirror of the round counter) for the table pot.
Desktop multiplayer stage sizing helpers.
Client-side completed-trick freeze timing hook; coordinated with server TRICK_PAUSE_MS minimum.
Transient multiplayer lobby error lifecycle hook.
Turn-error toast lifecycle hook for multiplayer actions.
Gateway connection wrapper for socket identity, auth/session context, and send/close state.
Connection registry and fanout/supersede coordination for active gateway clients.
Typed gateway event contracts used between gateway, hub, bus, and router code.
In-process server-event bus abstraction used with PostgreSQL fanout implementation.
Game update delivery helper for player-targeted snapshots/events.
Low-level WebSocket transport adapter for protocol parsing/sending and testable gateway behavior.
Display-id registry for stable public player identifiers and collision handling.
Durable reconnect-session abstraction backed by storage for multi-instance/session recovery foundations.
PostgreSQL room ownership lease helper for multi-instance room ownership foundations.
Server migration CLI entrypoint run after build with `npm run migrate --workspace apps/server`.
HTTP auth rate limiter utilities with proxy-aware IP derivation.
Bounded HTTP JSON body reader for auth routes and input validation boundary.
Public root export surface for core rules package.
Public multiplayer export surface for deterministic state-machine package entrypoint.
Seeded multiplayer game setup and player/deal initialization.
Deterministic timeout/bot bid helper for multiplayer decisions.
Deterministic timeout/bot move helper for multiplayer decisions.
Multiplayer inactivity/disconnect decision helpers and thresholds.
Public shared protocol package export surface.
Shared protocol error code/payload definitions.
Shared protocol version constant used by client handshake and server validation.
Browser layout regression coverage for responsive game/lobby surfaces.
Browser accessibility coverage for modal dialog behavior.
Browser resilience coverage for unavailable or malformed web storage.
Shared protocol schema/version validation tests.
Shared account title/loser-badge calculation tests.
Multiplayer deterministic RNG/shuffle coverage.
Multiplayer public/player snapshot privacy tests.
Multiplayer inactivity/disconnect behavior tests.
Gateway handshake/protocol/session behavior tests.
Room ownership routing tests for multi-instance lease boundaries.
Auth service unit coverage for account/profile/reset behavior.
HTTP auth route integration coverage for CORS, rate limits, bearer auth, and upload/reset flows.
Theme/default theme contract tests.
Shared Button primitive tests.

Standalone strong Domino Poker bot (hidden-hand, ISMCTS max^n). Explains architecture, the search/inference/bidding math, measured performance, and the AiClient integration surface. NOT yet wired into the live game.
Detailed integration guide for connecting the bot to the existing browser/server game.
Connection notes / hookup checklist for replacing the current AI bots with this package.
Root manifest of the ai_bot pnpm workspace (independent of the root npm workspaces; uses pnpm, not npm).
Declares the nested pnpm sub-packages (engine, ai, bot-adapter). This is a separate workspace tree; do not add it to the root npm workspaces.
Bot domain engine public API: tiles encoding, rules/legalMoves/trickWinner, scoring, GameState/PlayerView, seedable RNG. Pure and framework-free (mirrors but is independent of packages/core).
Bot intelligence public API: ISMCTS search, determinization/dealer, Monte Carlo bidding, inference (ConstraintTracker), rollout policy, opponent profiling. Imports only the bot engine; no I/O.
Information-Set Monte Carlo Tree Search (max^n) play engine: anytime budget, tree reuse, pondering, configurable reward (inclusion/points).

MAIN integration surface. AiClient = worker pool + postMessage<->Promise API (sync/requestBid/requestMove/ponder/dispose). The external game talks ONLY to this; it never imports ai/engine internals. Uses Node worker_threads; browser use needs a Web Worker swap (same protocol).
Zod-validated worker message protocol shared by AiClient and the worker; stable contract for Node worker_threads or a browser Web Worker.

Single authoritative source for gold-coin economy constants (STARTING_COINS, SP_REWARDS medium/hard/epic, POT_SPLIT 70/30, MIN_ENTRY_FEE, MAX_ENTRY_FEE sanity cap) and the splitPot helper (remainder→1st place, single human→100%). Imported by both server (enforces) and web (displays).
Gold-coin wallet storage interface (getBalance, atomic+idempotent applyLedger, sumLedgerSince) + isCoinStore guard. Implemented by both SqliteStorage and PostgresStorage.

Gold-coin money application layer over CoinStore: signup bonus (repair-on-read), getBalance, SP reward credit, creditSpRewardCapped (per-user lock + daily-cap clamp), and Phase 3 MP money — debitEntryFee (mp_entry, minBalance 0), refundEntryFee (mp_refund), payoutCoins (mp_payout); all reuse applyLedger (no new migration). The one place all balance changes flow through.

Phase 3 MP pot payout engine (mirrors OutcomeRecorder): caches pot + start-roster for paid matches at matchStarted; at gameOver splits the pot 70/30 (splitPot) among the top-2 registered, non-forfeited humans by standings (bots + forfeiters excluded), pays each via WalletService.payoutCoins (idempotent by matchId), and returns results for WALLET_UPDATED push. Fire-and-forget; wired in index.ts only when a wallet exists.

In-memory one-time SP reward tokens (D3 anti-cheat): issue snapshots the difficulty at game start; consume validates ownership/expiry and deletes (one-time). Per-user active limit + TTL. Single-instance.

Optional `/sp/*` HTTP routes: POST /sp/start (auth, issues a one-time token) and POST /sp/reward (auth, min duration, per-user rate limit, hard daily cap; reward derived from the token difficulty). Tried before the auth handler.
Shared raw-HTTP route helpers (writeJson, bearerToken, clientIp, applyCors) used by both authRoutes and spRewardRoutes to avoid duplication.
Reusable gold coin/chip SVG icon colored only via currentColor (inherits --coin), no color literals. Used by CoinBalance and the game-end reward banner.
Reusable gold balance display: CoinIcon + count-up number (requestAnimationFrame, animates only on change, respects prefers-reduced-motion). Token-colored via --coin.
Client HTTP wrapper for the SP coin-reward flow: apiSpStart (POST /sp/start) and apiSpReward (POST /sp/reward). Reuses requestJson/jsonInit from authApi.
Token-only CSS for the reusable coin UI primitives (.coinBalance/.coinBalanceIcon/.coinBalanceValue) and the game-end reward banner (.gameEndReward). Color only from --coin.

Deep-stats storage capability port (implemented by SqliteStorage + PostgresStorage): recordGameResult (idempotent by id), getPlayerGameStats (GROUP BY aggregate rows), getGameResultOwner (for /sp/complete replay). Includes assertValidGameResult, the DB-agnostic invariant guard backing migration 0008 CHECKs.

Player-stats application service: recordSpGame (builds sp:{gameToken} id), findSpGameOwner (replay), getStats which composes GROUP-BY aggregate rows into the UI shape (bid accuracy + sp placement-by-difficulty + mp placement).

Server-authoritative MP bid-accuracy writer (sibling of OutcomeRecorder, room-owner lifecycle). Accumulates from ROUND_RESULT.playerResults in onMatchEvents (before onMatchFinished), persists one idempotent mp:{matchId}:{userId} row per registered human at GAME_OVER. Records all MP games incl. with bots.
GET /stats (auth, own rate limiter, lazy) returning PlayerStatsService.getStats. Wired in httpServer before the auth handler; owned by the stats module, not auth.
Web client API + types for deep stats: apiGetStats (GET /stats) lazily fetched when the Statistics tab opens; PlayerStats/PlacementDistribution shapes mirror the server.

Profile dialog Statistics tab: lazy-fetches /stats and renders hand-rolled token-only CSS segmented bars (no chart lib, no animation, reduced-motion safe) for bid accuracy, tactical tendencies, and placement distributions by difficulty + MP. Loading/error/empty states.
